title: Local SEO Specialist – Google Business Profile (Podiatry Clinics | UK & AUS)
description:
Role Summary

We're a marketing agency working with Podiatrists across the UK and Australia. We're looking for a Local SEO Specialist who knows how to move the needle on Google Business Profiles and can communicate their work clearly and confidently.

This role is about results + communication. You'll be doing real SEO work using LeadSnap while keeping clients informed with fast, professional updates.

If you can't reply quickly and communicate clearly, this isn't the role for you.



Key Responsibilities



- Optimise and manage Google Business Profiles to improve local search rankings and attract new clients

- Leverage AI tools to identify optimisation opportunities across Google Business Profiles — categories, attributes, Q&A, services, descriptions, and more

- Generate high-quality AI images for GMB posts that look professional and on-brand for each clinic

- Use LeadSnap to track SEO performance, identify opportunities, and action improvements

- Spot when results are down and reach out to clients with context before they ask

- Send daily end-of-day updates so clients always know where things stand (WhatsAPP)

- Ask questions when something is unclear — don't guess



You're a Good Fit If You…



- Have hands-on experience optimising Google Business Profiles

- Understand local SEO signals — citations, categories, reviews, photos, geo-tagging, and more

- Are comfortable using AI tools in your day-to-day SEO workflow — not just aware of them, but actively using them to work faster and smarter

- Can explain SEO performance clearly to non-technical clients

- Are responsive and proactive 

- Take ownership and think like an operator, not a task-taker



Tools You'll Use



LeadSnap — SEO tracking and local search optimisation

Loom — Client update videos

WhatsApp — Day-to-day client communication

Google Analytics 



Payment & Bonuses



Pay review after 30 days for strong performers



To Apply

Record a 1–2 minute Loom video with your camera on covering:



Your name + location/timezone

Your experience with Google Business Profile and local SEO

A specific result you've achieved for a client (rankings, calls, impressions — real numbers if you have them)

A specific example of how you've used AI in your SEO or content workflow

A sample or example of AI-generated images you've created for a business (GMB posts, social, etc.)

Your availability and how fast you typically respond during work hours

Start your application with "Podiatrist" or it will not be considered.

title: Real Estate Acquisition Manager (U.S. Investor Leads) – Phone Sales Closer
description:
We are a fast-growing U.S.-based real estate investment company looking for an experienced Acquisition Manager to join our team.



Your role is simple:

Speak with motivated property owners

Build rapport

Analyze the deal

Present a cash offer

Get the property under contract



You will be speaking with warm + targeted seller leads such as:

Probate

Notice of Default / Pre-Foreclosure

Tax Delinquent

Absentee Owners

Distressed Property Owners

Vacant Property Owners



These are NOT cold random calls — these are targeted real estate lists.



Your Job



You will speak directly with U.S. homeowners and:

Build trust and uncover motivation

Gather property condition details

Determine seller timeline + needs

Evaluate property value

Present investor cash offers

Negotiate terms

Get verbal commitment

Send contract for signature



You are NOT just setting appointments…

You are closing deals over the phone.



Responsibilities



Call targeted seller lists daily

Have real conversations (not robotic scripts)

Ask smart questions about the condition & situation

Determine motivation

Evaluate if the property fits the buy box criteria

Use comps & simple valuation logic

Present investor-style offers

Handle objections

Move the seller to commitment

Get signed purchase agreements



You MUST Have Experience In:



Real estate acquisitions

U.S. seller conversations

Cash offer negotiation

Distressed property discussions

Running simple property analysis

Using comps (Zillow, Redfin, Propstream, etc)

Getting properties under contract remotely



This Job Is NOT For:



Appointment setters

Script readers

Cold-call beginners

Anyone without acquisition experience



If you have never locked up a deal, this is not the role.



You Should Be Comfortable:



Talking about property condition (repairs, updates, etc)

Discussing foreclosure or financial hardship

Talking through seller situations (probate, divorce, relocation, etc)

Negotiating respectfully but confidently

Making offers live on the call



Bonus If You Have Experience With:



Wholesaling

Creative finance

Pre-foreclosure conversations

Probate leads

Investor negotiation

CRM usage

Podio / GoHighLevel / REI systems



Tech Requirements



Strong English speaking ability

Reliable internet

Noise-free workspace

Experience using dialers / CRMs



Ability to work U.S. business hours



Compensation

We reward closers.

Comp includes:

Base pay

Performance bonuses

Per-deal incentive



Long-term opportunity for growth

(Top performers can earn significantly more)



Growth Opportunity



This is not just a job.



We are building a long-term acquisitions team, and future leadership roles will open for:

Lead Acquisition Manager

Disposition Support

Team Trainer



To Apply

Send:



A short voice recording introducing yourself

Your experience in real estate acquisitions

The number of deals you've helped get under contract

Your favorite type of seller leads to work

If you are a real closer who knows how to talk to motivated sellers and secure contracts…



We want to meet you.

title: Inside Sales Representative (ISA)

, and messaging platforms

title: Hybrid Graphic Designer & Video Editor
description:
We are seeking a versatile and creative Hybrid Graphic Designer & Video Editor who thrives in transforming ideas into compelling visual content across both graphic design and video platforms. You will play a crucial role in creating visually engaging graphics and captivating video content aligned with our brand’s storytelling and marketing objectives.



Role Description:

The Hybrid Graphic Designer & Video Editor will collaborate closely with the Visual Content Manager and marketing team to create impactful digital assets, ranging from static graphics to dynamic videos. You'll be responsible for ensuring consistency and quality across all visual media.



Duties and Responsibilities:

- Conceptualize, design, and execute graphics for social media, websites, ads, thumbnails, logos, branding materials, and marketing assets.

- Edit and produce engaging video content including YouTube long-form videos, shorts, Instagram Reels, TikToks, and marketing ads.

- Transform raw footage into visually engaging videos, enhancing them with text overlays, graphics, animations, motion graphics, and sound design.

- Ensure all graphics and videos maintain brand consistency and meet platform-specific guidelines.

- Manage multiple design and video projects simultaneously, from concept to delivery, meeting tight deadlines.

- Stay up-to-date with trends, AI tools, and best practices in graphic design, video editing, and social media.

- Actively revise and optimize content based on feedback and analytics.



Skills and Qualifications:

Technical Skills:

- Strong proficiency in graphic design tools: Canva and Adobe Photoshop required; Illustrator, InDesign, and Figma preferred.

- Proven expertise in video editing software: Adobe Premiere Pro, CapCut PC, or DaVinci Resolve required; Adobe After Effects experience preferred.

- Solid understanding of color theory, typography, visual storytelling, layout, and composition.

- Experience creating content optimized for various social media platforms and digital marketing channels.

- Familiarity with UX/UI principles, AI tools, and motion graphics is advantageous.

Soft Skills:

- Exceptional creativity with an eye for aesthetics and detail.

- Strong organizational and time management skills, with the ability to juggle multiple projects and meet deadlines.

- Excellent communication and collaboration skills, capable of effectively interacting with tea
mbers and stakeholders.

- Openness to constructive feedback and ability to proactively implement revisions.

- Independent initiative-taking and proactive problem-solving.



Qualifications:

- 3+ years of proven experience in graphic design and video editing, supported by a strong portfolio showcasing versatile projects.

- Demonstrated ability to translate creative briefs into compelling visual narratives and marketing content.

- Strong understanding of branding, visual storytelling, social media trends, and digital content optimization.



Our Core Values:

Client Wins: Fully committed to exceptional results, making client success our measure of achievement.

Integrity in Action: Honesty and transparency, addressing challenges directly and effectively.

Win-Win Discipline: Seeking solutions beneficial to both clients and the company through strategic thinking.

Team Synergy: Collaborative environment that values perspectives, feedback, and collective commitment.

Empowered Accountability: Encouraging ownership, independent problem-solving, and proactive responsibility.

Join our dynamic team and leverage your dual skillset to create outstanding visual content that drives impactful results.



To Apply, Submit your resume, Portfolio/Relevant Projects

title: Logsitics and Operations VA
description:
Job Opportunity – Remote | UK Time Zone



Excel Mastery  |  Freight Documentation  |  Bookkeeping  |  Executive Support



We are  UK-registered SME commodity trading company specialising in agri exports — primarily cashew nuts — across West Africa to international buyers in India, Vietnam, and China. We are a fast-growing, lean operation moving  hundreds of containers annually, and we run on precision, speed, and trust.

We work across multiple time zones and jurisdictions, and our team is small, intimate, intercultural,  sharp, and highly accountable. We are now looking for a talented remote VA to become a core part of our operations.



THE ROLE



This is a full-time remote position working flexible hours within the UK time zone (GMT/BST). You will be the operational backbone of the business — managing our shipment data, supporting executive communications, handling bookkeeping, and keeping our systems accurate and up to date.



This is not a passive role. We need someone who takes ownership, spots problems before they escalate, and brings order to a fast-moving trading environment.



We invite the candidate to take ownership and be creative and passionate about what the are doing,  curious and solution oriented. 



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES



Shipment Tracking & Documentation

Maintain and update our container shipment tracker daily

Manage export documentation: Bills of Lading, Commercial Invoices, Packing Lists, Certificates of Origin

Coordinate with carriers, freight forwarders, and suppliers on ETAs and status updates

Ensure documentation accuracy and compliance across multiple countries

Excel & Data Reporting

Build and maintain Excel-based tracking tools, dashboards, and reports

Advanced Excel skills required: VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, conditional formatting, data validation

Support Power BI reporting as we develop our operations platform

Maintain data integrity across all operational spreadsheets

Bookkeeping & Financial Administration

Maintain Excel-based bookkeeping records: income, expenses, invoices, and payments

Process and track supplier invoices and buyer payments

Reconcile transactions and flag discrepancies promptly

Support preparation of financial summaries for management review

Executive &
 Support

Manage and organise the CEO's inbox — prioritise, draft responses, and follow up

Coordinate communications with suppliers across West Africa and buyers in Asia

Draft professional correspondence, summaries, and reports

Support calendar management and scheduling across time zones



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Minimum 5 years experience in logistics coordination or freight forwarding

Advanced Excel proficiency — VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, data analysis are non-negotiable

Solid bookkeeping experience, ideally in a trading or import/export environment

Excellent written English — C1 or C2 level required

Proficient in Microsoft 365: Outlook, Excel, Word, Teams

Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy

Ability to work independently with minimal supervision

Experience with Bills of Lading, commercial invoices, and export documentation preferred

Reliable internet connection and dedicated remote working setup



WHAT WE OFFER



Full-time remote role — flexible hours within UK time zone (GMT/BST)

Paid trial period to ensure mutual fit before long-term commitment

Opportunity to grow with a fast-scaling international trading business

Direct working relationship with the CEO — your work will have real impact

Stable, ongoing role for the right candidate



Candidate Assessment

Please answer all questions honestly and in your own words. There are no trick questions — we are looking for clear thinking, practical experience, and good judgement. Take your time. Quality of response matters more than speed.

All responses should be written in English. Incomplete submissions will not be considered.



Q1.  A container of cashew nuts is booked from Abidjan, Ivory Coast to Europe.  We have sent out the PO, we need to  track and  confirms receipt of documents. What do you track, what do you check, and how do you make sure nothing falls through the cracks?

We are looking for: process knowledge, attention to detail, and understanding of international freight documentation.



Q2.  You are managing 12 active shipments simultaneously across 4 countries. It is Monday morning. How do you organise your day and prioritise your workload? What does your tracking system look like? How will you communicate the progress. 

We are looking for: organisation, systems thinking, and how you handle volume under pressure.



SECTION 2 — BOOKKEEPING & INVOICE MANAGEMENT

Q3.  Q3.  At Colibri Global Trading we work across multiple West African countries simultaneously — Guinea (GNF), Ivory Coast & Senegal (XOF), Ghana (GHS), Guinea-Bissau (XOF), Gambia, (GMD) and Tanzania (TZS) — alongside USD, GBP, and EUR. Suppliers send invoices, price confirmations, and payment details informally via WhatsApp voice notes, WhatsApp messages, and CamScanner document photos. How would you manage this? Walk us through your system for extracting, converting, recording, and reconciling financial data that arrives in this format. What tools would you use, how would you handle currency conversion, and how would you make sure nothing gets missed or miscalculated?



Q4.  Describe how you would set up a simple Excel-based bookkeeping tracker for a trading company that buys from African suppliers and sells to Asian buyers. What columns would you include? How would you handle multi-currency transactions?

We are looking for: practical Excel knowledge and financial organisation skills.



SECTION 3 — HANDLING MISTAKES & DATA ERRORS

Q5.  You have been maintaining the shipment tracker for two weeks. Your manager reviews it and finds that 3 containers have incorrect ETAs and a few are  missing entirely. How do you respond? What do you do in the next 30 minutes, and what do you put in place so it does not happen again?

We are looking for: accountability, problem-solving under pressure, and process improvement mindset.



Q6.  Tell us about a real mistake you made in a previous role involving data or documentation. What happened, what did you do about it, and what did you learn?

We are looking for: honesty, self-awareness, and the ability to learn from errors. ( we all make mistakes and we are always learning)



SECTION 4 — SUPPLIER & BUYER COMMUNICATION UNDER PRESSURE

Q7.  A buyer in India is chasing original shipping documents that were supposed to arrive 5 days ago. You contact the freight forwarder and discover the documents were sent to the wrong address. The buyer is threatening to reject the shipment. Draft the
 you would send to the buyer right now.

We are looking for: professional written English, composure under pressure, and customer communication skills.





SECTION 5 — SOP WRITING TASK

Task: Write a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)

Write a clear, step-by-step SOP for the following process:

"Inbox Management &
 Prioritisation for a Commodity Trading Business" — covering how to manage a busy CEO inbox, triage incoming
s, prioritise urgent supplier and buyer communications, draft responses, and ensure nothing is missed or delayed.

Your SOP should include: purpose, scope, step-by-step process, priority categories, escalation rules, and any tools or templates you would use. Write it as if handing it to a colleague on their first day.



SECTION 6 — REPORTING STRUCTURE & PROJECT COORDINATION

At Colibri Global Trading, we operate across multiple time zones simultaneously — our suppliers are in West Africa (Guinea, Ivory Coast, Senegal, Ghana, Guinea-Bissau, Tanzania), our buyers are in India, Vietnam, China, and the USA, EU , UK  and our headquarters is in the UK. Keeping everything visible, coordinated, and on track is essential.

We do not believe in micromanagement — but we do believe in full transparency and daily accountability. This section asks you to give us an idea that you would use to design the reporting system you would put in place if you were hired tomorrow.



Q9.  Design a daily reporting framework for this role. What would your start-of-day update look like? What would your end-of-day update include? How would you structure it so your manager always knows exactly what is moving, what is stuck, and what needs a decision — without having to ask?

We are looking for: proactive thinking, communication structure, and ownership of accountability. Show us a real format or template, not just a description.



Q10.  We are coordinating active shipments and supplier relationships across West Africa, while managing buyer communications in India, Vietnam, China, and the USA — all from a UK time zone base. How would you manage your day to ensure nothing falls through the gaps across these time zones? What tools or systems would you use?

We are looking for: time zone awareness, practical coordination skills, and systems thinking across a global operation.



Q11.  You are juggling 3 urgent tasks at the same time: a buyer in India is chasing documents, a supplier in Guinea has not confirmed a booking, and the weekly shipment tracker needs updating before a 2pm call. Walk us through exactly how you handle the next 90 minutes.

We are looking for: prioritisation under pressure, decisiveness, and practical task management.



Q12.  What do you need from a manager to do your best work? How do you prefer to receive feedback, flag problems, and escalate issues? Be honest — we want to know how you actually work, not what sounds good.

We are looking for: self-awareness, communication maturity, and cultural fit with a lean, fast-moving team.



Thank you for completing this questionnaire.

Please return this completed document along with your CV and a brief covering note explaining why you feel you are the right fit for Colibri Global Trading.

title: Outsourced CFO / Controller — US Healthcare Company (Remote, Philippines)
description:
Job Title: Outsourced CFO / Controller — US Healthcare Company (Remote, Philippines)

Company: Remote Imaging Consultants (RIC)

Industry: Healthcare / Teleradiology (US-based)

Location: Remote — Philippines-based (Cebu preferred, Manila also welcome)

Engagement Type: Independent Contractor (No PH employment benefits)

Reports To: Company Owner (Physician-Entrepreneur, based in Hawaii)

Hours: Must be available during US business hours for key tasks (flexible schedule otherwise; Hawaii is UTC–10)

Equipment: Company-provided laptop + software licenses



ABOUT THE COMPANY

Remote Imaging Consultants (RIC) is a US-based teleradiology company providing radiology reading services to hospitals and imaging centers across the United States. We manage a network of radiologist contractors and develop proprietary cloud-based PACS and AI-powered clinical tools. We have operations in Hawaii with radiologist contractors in multiple US states, and a technology team in Taiwan and the Philippines.



RIC is currently structured as an S-Corporation and may transition to a different entity type (C-Corp or limited partnership) in the near future. We need someone who can grow with us through that transition.



ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for a skilled, reliable finance professional based in Cebu to serve as our outsourced CFO / Controller on a contractor basis. You will own the day-to-day financial operations of the company: bookkeeping, US tax preparation, financial reporting, and coordination with US-based tax professionals (CPA and/or tax attorney) for complex filings and entity planning.



This is a high-trust, high-autonomy role reporting directly to the owner. We want someone who is excellent at what they do, takes initiative, and communicates clearly.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1) Bookkeeping and Accounting

- Maintain the company’s books using US accounting software (QuickBooks Online, Xero, or equivalent — open to your recommendation)

- Reconcile bank accounts, credit cards, and payment processor accounts monthly

- Process accounts payable and accounts receivable

- Track contractor payments (1099 recipients) and manage W-9 collection

- Manage payroll processing for US-based employees



2) US Tax Preparation and Compliance

- Prepare US federal and state corporate tax returns (currently Form 1120-S), or coordinate with a US-based CPA to ensure accurate and timely filing

- Prepare the owner’s personal federal and state tax returns (Form 1040), or coordinate preparation with a US-based CPA

- Calculate and file quarterly estimated tax payments (corporate and personal)

- Prepare and file 1099-NEC forms for all contractors annually

- Manage multi-state tax compliance: Hawaii (home state), Oregon (Corporate Activity Tax), California (franchise tax), and additional states as needed

- Coordinate with a US-based CPA or tax attorney for complex filings, IRS representation, and entity structuring as needed



3) Financial Reporting and Analysis

- Produce monthly financial statements (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow)

- Provide wRVU-based productivity and revenue analysis for radiologist contractors

- Develop and maintain financial dashboards and KPIs

- Support budgeting, forecasting, and cash flow planning



4) Strategic Support

- Advise on contractor compensation structures and client pricing strategy

- Support the owner and US-based tax professionals during any entity restructuring (S-Corp to C-Corp or LP, potential reincorporation in South Dakota or Delaware)

- Assist with financial aspects of new client contracts and negotiations



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Philippine CPA license (active and in good standing)

- Minimum 3 years of hands-on experience with US tax preparation and compliance (1120-S, 1040, state filings, 1099s)

- Proficient in US accounting software (QuickBooks Online, Xero, or equivalent)

- Previous experience working with US clients, either in a US-facing BPO or directly

- Strong understanding of US GAAP and IRS tax code as it applies to small businesses and S-Corporations

- Excellent English (written and verbal) — must communicate clearly with the owner, US attorneys, and financial institutions

- Reliable high-speed internet and a professional home office setup

- Based in the Philippines (Cebu preferred — the owner travels there regularly and other team members are based there, but Manila or elsewhere in the Philippines is fine for the right candidate)



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- US CPA license or US CPA exam passer (strongly preferred)

- PTIN (Preparer Tax Identification Number) holder

- Experience with healthcare or physician-owned businesses

- Familiarity with entity conversions (S-Corp ? C-Corp or LP)

- Experience coordinating with US tax attorneys on entity structuring

- International tax awareness (FBAR, FATCA, Form 2555, foreign income reporting)

- Big 4 or mid-tier BPO background (SGV/EY, Isla Lipana/PwC, or similar)

- Familiarity with 1099 contractor management and wRVU-based compensation models



COMPENSATION

This is an independent contractor engagement. No employment benefits (PhilHealth, SSS, Pag-IBIG, 13th month) are included — you are responsible for your own taxes and contributions.

- Monthly Rate: ?50,000 – ?80,000/month (~$860 – $1,400 USD), depending on experience

- Engagement Type: Independent contractor (no employment benefits)

- Schedule: Flexible; must overlap with US business hours for key tasks (Hawaii is UTC–10)

- Growth: Opportunity to grow into a strategic CFO role as the company scales



HOW TO APPLY

Send your resume/CV, a brief cover letter highlighting your US tax experience, and at least two professional references to BOTH:

- Joy (Recruitment Coordinator): 

- Henry (Operations): 

Subject line: CFO Application – RIC

title: Full-Time Operations Manager (Short-Term and Mid-Term Rentals)
description:
Location: Remote (PH preferred)

Schedule: Full-time with coverage across 8am–10pm ET (rotating), on-call escalation as needed

Compensation: Competitive monthly + KPI bonus

Reports to: CEO

Tools: Guesty, QuickBooks Online, ClickUp, Slack, Google Workspace



Mission

- Own daily operations and lead the remote ops team to deliver zero missed cleans, 98% turnover pass rate while keeping owners informed and delighted.



Leadership Scope

- Directly manage: Ops Associate/Guest Experience Associates (as hired), cleaners/vendors, and Field QA (local, PT).

- Partner closely with Systems Manager on PMS automations and reporting.



Core Responsibilities

- Guest Operations: Monitor inbox SLAs; resolve issues; coach team on tone and macros; handle escalations calmly.

- Turnovers & QC: Ensure cleans are assigned at booking; T-48/T-12 confirmations; enforce photo-proof and re-clean workflows; coordinate Field QA spot checks.

- Vendor Management: Recruit/scorecard cleaners and handymen; negotiate rates; maintain surge coverage for events; enforce SOP compliance.

- Maintenance & Incidents: Create/track work orders; dispatch techs; manage smart lock access; drive fast MTTR.

- PMS & Automations (Guesty): Maintain tasks, tags, pipelines, templates; keep calendars/data pristine; collaborate on pricing rule execution.

- ClickUp Ops Hub: Maintain boards, SLAs, and risk logs; run daily standup and end-of-day audit; close loops.

- Reporting & Owner Updates: Publish daily ops digest; weekly KPI scorecard (SLAs, turnover pass/re-clean %, incidents); contribute to monthly owner reports.

- SOPs & Training: Build and iterate SOPs; onboard/train new ops staff; lead post-incident reviews and improvements.



Required Experience (Must-Haves)

- 2+ years leading STR operations for a property manager or co-host (multi-unit portfolio required).

- Deep hands-on with Guesty (or Hostfully/OwnerRez) inbox, automations, tasks, and calendar hygiene.

- Proven track record coordinating cleaners/vendors with photo-based QC and re-clean workflows.

- Strong written English; guest-centric communication; stakeholder management with owners.

- Process discipline: checklists, deadlines, evidence-based task closure; comfortable with metrics.

- Proficient in ClickUp (or Asana/Monday) and Google Sheets dashboards.



Nice-to-Haves

- Smart locks (Yale/Schlage/August), noise monitoring (Minut/NoiseAware), and basic PriceLabs competence.

- Built on-call schedules and incident playbooks; experience with compliance/permits.

- Familiarity with owner statements and clean ops ? QuickBooks Online handoff.



Success Metrics (first 90 days)

- 0 missed cleanings; re-clean rate 98%.

- Guest response times 95%; weekly ops report cadence; vendor scorecards implemented.



Universal Traits & Culture

-  STR industry operators; hospitality-obsessed; proactive communicators.

-  Own outcomes; improve SOPs; document work; protect owner assets and guest experience.

-  Reliable tech + backup internet/power; comfortable with security and privacy standards.



Hiring Process & Work Sample

- 30-min interview

- 90-min paid simulation (inbox triage, assign turnovers, write owner update, create daily digest)

- References.

title: Architectural Draftsperson / ArchiCAD Specialist
description:
Overall Purpose and Objective of This Position:

The Architectural Draftsperson / BIM Specialist is a remote, specialist role designed for an experienced ArchiCAD practitioner who combines a strong eye for design with a thorough, practical understanding of technical documentation.

Central to this role is the implementation of sustainable building practices that elevate thermal performance and reduce environmental impact. Working within a design-and-build practice, this position plays a vital role in producing high-quality 3D renders, coordinating working drawings, and supporting project administration and preconstruction workflows to ensure projects are buildable, accurate, and compliant.



Accountabilities

1. Architectural Drafting & 3D Visualisation

Objective: Produce expert-level models and renders to translate design intent into accurate, buildable documentation. Responsibilities:

Produce expert-level 3D models and renders in ArchiCAD for design development, client presentations, and planning submissions.

Develop and maintain coordinated BIM models to a high standard throughout all project stages.

Collaborate with the design team to ensure documentation reflects intended design and sustainability goals.

2. Technical Documentation

Objective: Prepare detailed, high-quality working drawings that comply with all Australian and Victorian regulatory standards. Responsibilities:

Prepare detailed working drawings including architectural plans, sections, elevations, and construction details.

Produce high-quality technical details for building permit and construction purposes.

Ensure all documentation complies with the National Construction Code (NCC), Rescode, and all applicable Victorian building regulations.

Coordinate with structural and services consultants to integrate documentation effectively.

3. Sustainability & Thermal Performance

Objective: Support the integration of sustainable building strategies and passive design principles into all project documentation. Responsibilities:

Detail insulation, thermal mass, glazing performance, and airtightness specifications.

Support the integration of passive design principles into technical drawings.

Contribute to the continuous improvement of sustainable design and construction processes.

4. Project & Preconstruction Administration

Objective: Ensure accurate and consistent project data and documentation control to support the project lifecycle. Responsibilities:

Maintain drawing registers, manage document version control, and coordinate the issue of documentation.

Prepare and distribute correspondence, transmittals, and meeting minutes.

Track RFIs, variations, and other project communications in an organised manner.

Support the preparation of schedules of finishes and specification documents.

Conduct thorough drawing reviews for accuracy and detail.



Expectations

The Architectural Draftsperson / BIM Specialist is expected to:

Work proactively to assist the team with accurate, timely, and compliant technical documentation.

Maintain clean, consistent, and up-to-date BIM models and drawing registers across all systems.

Communicate professionally, clearly, and responsively with the Australian-based design and construction teams.

Demonstrate a high level of independent task management and accountability in a remote environment.

Take ownership of learning related to evolving Victorian building regulations and sustainable construction technologies.



Characteristics and Skill-Sets

Exceptional Attention to Detail: Highly accurate across all aspects of documentation, technical detailing, and correspondence.

ArchiCAD Expertise: Expert-level proficiency; genuinely at the top of software capabilities.

Regulatory Knowledge: Practical understanding of Victorian building regulations, the NCC, and relevant Australian Standards.

Construction Knowledge: Solid experience in technical working drawings for residential construction and design-build workflows.

Tech-Savviness: Competent with ArchiCAD rendering (or linked workflows like Twinmotion/Lumion) and project management platforms.

Sustainability Focus: Knowledge of passive design strategies and thermal performance tools.



Reporting Channel:

The Head of Design is your direct leader and will provide direction and support as required.



Application Requirements

Please record a short video when submitting your application answering the following questions:

What similar experience do you have with ArchiCAD and Victorian residential projects?

What is your greatest technical or BIM-related skill?

What is your home office and hardware setup?

What part of the design and documentation process do you love most?

What You’ll Be Responsible For:



- Initiating conversations with new followers, story viewers, and engaged prospects

- Reaching out to warm and targeted outbound leads daily

- Using our scripts + frameworks while making conversations feel natural and human

- Asking intelligent qualifying questions

- Moving qualified prospects into booked sales calls

- Following up consistently with warm leads

- Tracking conversations and outcomes inside our CRM

- Maintaining our brand voice and communication standards

- You will be expected to generate volume while maintaining quality.



What We’re Looking For:

title: Junior Local SEO Specialist
description:
About Paint & Profits



Paint & Profits is a marketing agency that helps local service businesses grow through SEO and digital marketing. Most of our clients are painting contractors, along with other companies in the home improvement and home services industry. Our work focuses heavily on local SEO, helping these businesses rank in their service areas, generate more calls and quote requests, and consistently grow their pipeline of jobs.



Hours: 40 hrs/week (8 hrs/day)

Compensation: Competitive (based on experience) + performance growth path

Start: ASAP

Tools required: Time Doctor + daily logbook + weekly reporting



What you’ll do



You will help improve local rankings and website performance for service-area and location-based businesses. Your work will be hands-on, tactical, and measured weekly.



Core responsibilities

-Keyword research focused on local intent (city + service, neighborhood intent, “near me,” etc.)

-Find content gaps and opportunities (services missing, location pages missing, weak pages that need upgrades)

-Create and optimize hyper-local pages (service + city pages done right, not spam)

-Write and update content (blogs, service pages, FAQs) with strong on-page SEO

-Rewrite underperforming pages to improve rankings and conversions

-Improve on-page SEO: titles, headings, internal linking, schema basics, content structure

-Track keywords and report on progress weekly (what changed, what you shipped, what’s next)

-Identify and document issues holding sites back (technical basics, indexation, cannibalization, weak internal linking, poor page intent match)



What “good” looks like here



-You can explain why a keyword is worth targeting, and what page should target it

-You can find gaps quickly and turn them into a clean 90-day plan

-You ship consistently and keep a clean record (Time Doctor + daily logbook)

-You think local-first (maps/local intent), not national SEO or ecommerce playbooks



Requirements



-Fluent English (written + spoken)

-Comfortable writing and editing pages (clear, human writing)

-Strong keyword research skills (not just pulling lists, but choosing winners)

-Basic working knowledge of Google Search Console + GA4

-Organized: logbooks, weekly reporting, monthly goals



Nice to have



-Experience with service businesses (home services, local professional services, etc.)

-Experience with WordPress or similar CMS

-Basic understanding of technical SEO fundamentals (indexing, canonicals, duplicates, internal linking)



How we manage work (non-negotiable)



-Time Doctor tracking during work hours

-Daily logbook (what you did, time spent, output links)

-Weekly summary report to your manager

-Monthly goals with clear deliverables



To apply



Send:

-Your resume or LinkedIn

-A short note describing your local SEO experience

-1 example: a page you improved or a plan you created

title: Grade 5 Math Tutor (Remote)
description:
Location: Remote (PST Time Zone)

Schedule: 5:00 PM PST, 1-hour session per day (Monday - Thursday)



Job Description:

We are looking for a reliable and enthusiastic Grade 5 Math Tutor to work with a 10-year-old student. The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of Grade 5 math concepts, including arithmetic, fractions, decimals, geometry, and introductory algebra.



The tutor will be responsible for helping the student improve their math skills by explaining concepts, providing practice problems, and supporting their overall academic development.



Responsibilities:

Provide 1-hour tutoring sessions focused on Grade 5 math topics, Monday through Thursday.

Explain math concepts in a clear, patient, and engaging manner.

Assist with homework and practice problems.

Adapt teaching methods to fit the student’s learning style and pace.

Track progress and give feedback on performance.

Foster a positive and encouraging learning environment.



Requirements:

Experience tutoring math at the elementary school level (preferably Grade 5).

Strong knowledge of the U.S. Grade 5 Math Curriculum (arithmetic, fractions, decimals, geometry, and basic algebra).

Reliable and consistent availability for 1-hour sessions at 5:00 PM PST, Monday through Thursday.

Excellent communication skills with the ability to work effectively with children.

A positive and patient attitude toward teaching.



Compensation:

Starting rate: $6/hour.

Rate increase to $7/hour after 30 days of successful tutoring.



How to Apply:

If you're passionate about helping a student succeed in math and meet the above qualifications, please apply by sending:



- Your resume.

- A brief description of your tutoring experience.

- A video link introduction telling us about your teaching style and why you would be a good fit for this role.

- A portfolio (if applicable), including any reviews from past clients, and relevant materials or examples of previous tutoring work.

- Include the word "MDN" when submitting an application to be considered. For example: Grade 5 Math Tutor - MDN



We look forward to hearing from you!

title: AI OPERATIONS & CLIENT AUTOMATION SPECIALIST (REMOTE – PHILIPPINES)
description:
ABOUT US

We are a specialised AI and messaging automation agency building stable, well-governed automation systems for businesses using messaging platforms, AI agents, and workflow automation.

This is not a volume agency and not a generic VA setup.



ABOUT THE ROLE

This role focuses on client automation setup and optimisation, AI agent configuration, testing, and adjustments, monitoring live systems, and handling i
 calmly. It also includes clear escalation to internal leads and platform support teams when required.



This is not a traditional customer support or inbox management role.

You will not be responding to end-customer enquiries or managing live chat queues.

Your focus is on supporting and maintaining client automation systems.

It is an operations and systems role involving logic, judgement, and responsibility.



You will receive structured on-the-job training and SOPs, but you must be comfortable learning and thinking independently.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



CLIENT SETUP & SYSTEM ADJUSTMENTS

Configure messaging platforms, automations, and AI agents

Implement changes based on documented instructions

Review existing setups and flag potential issues or improvements

Ensure agents behave safely and within defined rules



MONITORING & I
 HANDLING

Monitor for unexpected agent behaviour or automation failures

Triage issues methodically without panic

Apply containment actions such as pausing flows or triggering human takeover

Escalate issues with clear evidence and summaries



COMMUNICATION & DOCUMENTATION

Provide clear written updates during i
 or changes

Translate technical issues into simple explanations

Document changes, i
, and resolutions clearly



WORKING EXPECTATIONS

This is a remote, work-from-home role

On-the-job training will be provided

You are expected to self-learn platform features and documentation

Occasionally, urgent issues may arise outside standard hours. These are not daily and follow a clear escalation process

You are not expected to solve everything, but you are expected to contain issues, document clearly, and escalate correctly



REQUIRED TRAITS

We care more about how you think than years of experience.



You should think logically and systematically

Stay calm when problems are unclear

Communicate clearly in writing

Take ownership without overstepping

Be comfortable saying ‘I am not sure yet, but here is what I am checking’



REQUIRED SKILLS

Strong written English

Comfort using web-based SaaS platforms

Logical problem-solving ability

Ability to follow and improve SOPs

Willingness to learn AI agents and automation systems



NICE TO HAVE (NOT REQUIRED)

Experience with chat platforms or messaging systems

Experience with automation tools like 
 or Zapier

Background in technical support, QA, or operations



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

Not a sales role

Not a call centre or live chat role

Not a purely repetitive task role



This role requires judgement and responsibility.



COMPENSATION

Monthly salary: USD 800 to 1,000 depending on experience

Fully remote

Long-term role with growth as responsibilities expand



HOW TO APPLY

Please apply with your CV and a short written explanation answering this question:

If an automation suddenly stops working late at night, how would you approach the problem?



We are looking at how you think, not perfect answers.



HIRING TIMELINE

This role is being recruited in December.

Shortlisted candidates will be confirmed before the year end.

Official onboarding and work will begin in January 2026, after the holiday period.

No work is expected during December.

description:
About the Role

We’re looking for a creative, high-energy Social Media Marketer to help take Phillip’s message of transformation to a global audience. Your mission is to turn powerful speaking moments, personal stories, and the principles from "Enjoy the Journey" into viral, engaging content that inspires growth and drives bookings.



What You’ll Do

Content Creation: Edit and post high-impact short-form videos (Reels, TikToks, Shorts) from Phillip’s speaking engagements and daily life.

Growth Strategy: Develop and execute plans to increase reach and followers across Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn.

Community Building: Engage with our audience—reply to comments, manage DMs, and foster a community of people seeking personal growth.

Platform Management: Tailor content for different platforms—inspiring stories for IG/TikTok and professional insights for LinkedIn to attract event planners.

Analytics: Track what’s working and pivot the strategy based on data and trends.



What We’re Looking For

A Storyteller: You know how to take a raw video and turn it into a story that stops the scroll.

Platform Expert: You live on social media and stay ahead of the latest trends and algorithm changes.

Video Skills: Strong mobile or desktop video editing skills (CapCut, Adobe Premiere, etc.).

Vibe Fit: You’re passionate about personal development and believe in the power of authenticity.

Proactive: You don’t wait for instructions; you bring fresh ideas to the table every week.

Why Join Us?

You’ll be working directly with an international speaker to change lives. We’re a fast-moving, authentic brand that values "living life to the fullest" while working hard.

title: Real Estate Lead List Builder / Public Records Research VA
description:
About Us



Polus Acquisitions Group is a U.S.-based real estate investment company focused on acquiring off-market properties directly from homeowners. We specialize in identifying motivated sellers through public records and government data, building relationships with property owners, and negotiating contracts that allow us to purchase properties quickly.



Our team operates remotely and collaborates closely across several roles including data research, lead generation, marketing, and acquisitions. The success of our acquisitions team depends heavily on the quality and consistency of the data we generate.



We are looking for a Real Estate Data Manager / Lead Generation Specialist who understands how to navigate U.S. county public records, generate property owner leads, and manage those leads throughout our system until they are ready for our acquisitions team.



This is a long-term position with growth opportunities for someone who is reliable, detail-oriented, and capable of working as part of a team.



Compensation



Base Salary: $400 per month



Paid bi-weekly (every two weeks).



In addition to base salary, bonuses are paid when contracts are closed from leads you generated and managed.



Bonus structure follows the same performance tiers used by our acquisitions team and rewards consistent production.



Bonuses are applied once contracts are successfully signed and closed from leads that originated from your data and outreach work.



This means your performance and accuracy directly contribute to the company’s growth and your earning potential.



Schedule



Full-time position



Monday – Friday

40 hours per week



Some overlap with U.S. business hours may be required depending on workflow and communication with the team.



How Our Company Operates



Our company operates on a data-driven lead generation system that focuses on identifying property owners who may be motivated to sell.



Our process begins with public records research, where we identify property owners through county government databases. These records are then organized, verified, and prepared for outreach.



Once leads are generated, our marketing systems reach out to those property owners through SMS campaigns and phone calls. If a homeowner is interested in selling, the lead is passed to our acquisitions team who negotiates and closes the deal.



Your role is critical in ensuring that the entire pipeline runs smoothly and consistently produces high-quality leads.



What You Will Be Doing

County Public Records Research



You will regularly navigate U.S. county websites to locate property records and government filings.



Examples include:



• Probate filings

• Tax delinquent properties

• Pre-foreclosures / foreclosure filings

• Code violations

• Property transfers

• Evictions



These records are used to identify property owners who may be motivated to sell.



You must be comfortable navigating:



• County clerk websites

• District clerk court records

• Property appraisal districts

• Tax assessor databases

• Recorder / deed records



Lead Data Collection



Once records are located, you will gather and organize key information including:



• Property address

• Owner name

• Mailing address

• Parcel / APN number

• Filing information (when applicable)



Accuracy is extremely important because this data is used by our marketing and acquisitions teams.



Data Cleaning & Organization



You will prepare datasets by:



• Removing duplicates

• Standardizing addresses

• Verifying ownership information

• Formatting spreadsheets for CRM import



The goal is to produce clean, reliable data that can be used for marketing campaigns.



Skip Tracing



After property owner data is collected, you will assist with skip tracing to locate contact information for property owners.



This may include:



• Phone numbers

•
 addresses

• Additional ownership data



Tools used may include:



• SmartSkip

• BatchSkipTracing

• TruePeopleSearch

• Other skip tracing tools



SMS Lead Generation



Once leads are prepared, they are uploaded into our CRM and marketing systems.



From there we conduct SMS outreach campaigns to contact property owners and determine whether they are interested in selling.



You may assist with:



• Preparing lead lists for SMS campaigns

• Managing responses from property owners

• Organizing conversations in the CRM

• Identifying interested sellers



Lead Management



If a property owner expresses interest in selling, the lead must be carefully documented and passed to the acquisitions team.



This includes:



• Updating the CRM with accurate notes

• Organizing seller information

• Communicating updates to the acquisitions team



Your role is to ensure no leads are lost and every opportunity is tracked correctly.



What It Takes to Succeed Here

Attention to Detail



The entire operation relies on clean data. Small mistakes in records can cause major problems later in the process.



Consistency



Our marketing systems depend on a steady supply of leads. We need someone who can reliably produce new data each week.



Problem Solving



County websites can sometimes be difficult to navigate. You must be capable of researching and figuring out how to locate the necessary information.



Team Communication



We operate as a team. Clear communication and updates are essential so that everyone understands what is happening in the pipeline.



You must be comfortable collaborating with:



• Data team members

• Lead managers

• Acquisitions specialists



Requirements



• Experience researching U.S. county public records

• Strong Excel or Google Sheets skills

• Experience cleaning and organizing data

• Familiarity with real estate data or lead generation

• Understanding of property ownership records

• Ability to manage and track leads in a CRM

• Reliable internet and quiet work environment

• Strong English communication skills



Ideal Candidate



The best candidate will:



• Have experience working with real estate investors or wholesalers

• Understand how motivated seller lead generation works

• Be highly organized and detail-oriented

• Be proactive and reliable

• Be comfortable working with large datasets



Why Join Our Team



This position provides an opportunity to become part of a growing real estate investment company and play a key role in generating the opportunities that drive our business.



You will gain valuable experience in:



• Real estate investing

• Lead generation systems

• Data management

• Property research



Top performers will have opportunities for long-term growth and increased responsibilities within the company.



How to Apply (Important)



To apply, please follow these instructions carefully.




 your application to: 



Subject Line: Real Estate Data Manager Application – OJPH



Attach your resume with relevant experience



Include a 1–2 minute video or audio introduction explaining your experience with real estate data or public records research



At the top of your application, include the words:



“DATA PIPELINE”



Applications that do not follow these instructions may not be considered.

title: Real Estate Lead List Builder / Public Records Research VA (Remote)
description:
Property Data Research & Lead Generation Specialist (Remote)

About Us



Polus Acquisitions Group is a U.S.-based real estate investment company focused on acquiring off-market properties directly from homeowners. Our business is driven by data and research. We identify motivated sellers through public records, government filings, and property ownership databases.



Our team operates remotely and collaborates across several departments including acquisitions, lead management, and data research.



We are currently looking for a Property Data Research & Lead Generation Specialist who can help generate, organize, and manage property owner data that fuels our marketing and acquisitions pipeline.



Compensation



Base Salary: $400 per month



Paid bi-weekly (every two weeks).



Additional bonuses are paid when contracts close from leads you generated and managed.



The more accurate and consistent your lead generation work is, the greater the opportunity to earn bonuses.



Schedule



Full-time position



Monday – Friday

40 hours per week



Some overlap with U.S. business hours may be required for team communication.



How Our System Works



Our company uses a data-driven lead generation system designed to identify homeowners who may be motivated to sell their property.



The process starts with public records research, where we locate property owners through county government databases and court filings.



After the data is collected, it is cleaned, organized, and verified before being uploaded into our CRM. From there, our marketing systems send SMS messages and calls to property owners to determine if they are interested in selling.



Interested sellers are then passed to our acquisitions team who negotiates and closes contracts.



Your role is essential in ensuring that high-quality data consistently enters our system.



Your Responsibilities

Public Records Research



You will research and collect property data from U.S. county websites such as:



• County Clerk websites

• District Clerk court systems

• Property appraisal districts

• Tax assessor databases

• Recorder / deed records



Examples of records you may pull include:



• Probate filings

• Tax delinquent properties

• Pre-foreclosures / foreclosure filings

• Code violations

• Property transfers



Data Collection & Verification



You will gather and verify information such as:



• Property address

• Owner name

• Mailing address

• Parcel / APN number

• Filing details



Accuracy is extremely important because this data feeds directly into our marketing system.



Data Cleaning & Organization



You will prepare lists by:



• Removing duplicates

• Standardizing address formats

• Verifying property ownership

• Preparing spreadsheets for CRM import



You should be comfortable working with large spreadsheets and datasets.



Skip Tracing



You will assist with finding contact information for property owners using skip tracing tools.



This includes locating:



• Phone numbers

•
 addresses

• Additional ownership information



Lead Management & Marketing Support



After data is prepared, it is uploaded into our system where outreach campaigns begin.



You may assist with:



• Preparing SMS marketing lists

• Organizing responses from property owners

• Updating the CRM with lead notes

• Passing qualified leads to the acquisitions team



What We Are Looking For



• Strong research skills

• Experience working with spreadsheets and data

• Attention to detail and organization

• Ability to navigate websites and find information

• Strong communication and teamwork skills



Requirements



• Experience with Excel or Google Sheets

• Ability to research and collect data online

• Reliable internet connection

• Strong English communication skills

• Ability to follow structured processes



Experience with real estate, property research, or lead generation is highly preferred.



Why Join Us



This role gives you the opportunity to work directly with a growing U.S. real estate investment company and gain experience in real estate lead generation, property research, and data systems.



Strong performers will have opportunities for long-term growth and additional responsibilities.



How to Apply



To apply, please follow these instructions carefully.



Send your application to 



Subject Line: Lead Generation Specialist Application – OJPH



Attach your resume



Include a short 1–2 minute audio or video introduction



At the top of your application include the phrase:



“REAL ESTATE DATA”



Applications that do not follow these instructions may not be reviewed.

mbers.

ing e-commerce platforms (e.g., Refund/Return disputes, customer complaints, and fraud prevention).

title: Client Success + Bonuses (Loom Voice Updates (not video) + WhatsApp) – Marketing Agency
description:
Role Summary



We’re a marketing agency working with Podiatrists accoss the UK and Aus and we need a VA who can communicate clearly, summarise information fast, and send professional client updates using Loom voice recordings and WhatsApp.



This role is mainly about communication + execution, not “admin busywork”.

You’ll act like a proactive assistant who helps things move forward without needing constant follow-ups.





Key Responsibilities



- Record Loom voice updates to explain tasks, results, and next steps

- Organise our website developer to make small website changes

- Send WhatsApp updates to clients in a clean, confident, professional tone

- Organise information into simple checklists and next actions

- Ask questions when something is unclear (instead of guessing)

- Proactive in quickly replying to the clients on WhatsApp

- Must understand when a client might need extra support if results are down

- Must be able to send a daily end-of-day updates





Payment Bonus:



- You get a bonus based on how long you keep clients

- You get a bonus if you upsell customers

- A pay review will happen after 30 days if you perform well



You’re a Good Fit If You…



- Speak and write clear, fluent English

- Can explain things simply and confidently

- Take initiative and think like an operator

- Are reliable with deadlines and follow instructions closely



Tools you will use:



- Loom

- WhatsApp

- ClickUp

- Google Sheets



Please complete to finalise the submission:



Short Loom introducing yourself (1–2 mins) with the camera on

1. Your name + location/timezone

2. Your experience supporting businesses/clients

3. Why you’re a fit for a communication-first VA role

4. Your availability + how fast you can reply during work hours

title: Client Success + Bonuses (Loom Voice Updates (not video) + WhatsApp) – Marketing Agency
description:
Role Summary



We’re a marketing agency working with Podiatrists accoss the UK and Aus and we need a VA who can communicate clearly, summarise information fast, and send professional client updates using Loom voice recordings and WhatsApp.



This role is mainly about communication + execution, not “admin busywork”.

You’ll act like a proactive assistant who helps things move forward without needing constant follow-ups.





Key Responsibilities



- Record Loom voice updates to explain tasks, results, and next steps

- Organise our website developer to make small website changes 

- Send WhatsApp updates to clients in a clean, confident, professional tone

- Organise information into simple checklists and next actions

- Ask questions when something is unclear (instead of guessing)

- Proactive in quickly replying to the clients on WhatsApp

- Must understand when a client might need extra support if results are down

- Must be able to send a daily end-of-day updates





Payment Bonus:



- You get a bonus based on how long you keep clients 

- You get a bonus if you upsell customers

- A pay review will happen after 30 days if you perform well



You’re a Good Fit If You…



- Speak and write clear, fluent English

- Can explain things simply and confidently

- Take initiative and think like an operator

- Are reliable with deadlines and follow instructions closely



Tools you will use:



- Loom

- WhatsApp

- ClickUp

- Google Sheets



Please complete to finalise the submission:



Short Loom introducing yourself (1–2 mins) with the camera on

 1.     Your name + location/timezone

 2. Your experience supporting businesses/clients

 3. Why you’re a fit for a communication-first VA role

 4. Your availability + how fast you can reply during work hours



- Start the application with "Dog", or you won’t be considered.

- Please follow the Loom video linked below, as you will be recording a variant of this.

It doesn’t need to be word-for-word — just match the same structure and key points. Please have the camera off for this. 



When you record your Loom, please make sure you have the website open on-screen:





Loom reference video:





We will start off with a paid trial if we are happy with your application

title: Short Term Rental (STR)/ Airbnb Guest Services Coordinator/Can Lead to Management
description:
NOTE: Your application will not be considered if you do not have experience in short term rental management and/or experience working directly for Airbnb. Please do not apply without one of those types of experience. Be sure to mention the type of experience you have in the subject heading. You must use one of the following titles in the subject heading of your application message to be considered for this position--either "STR Experience" (if you worked for a company that manages short term rentals, "Airbnb Experience" if you worked for the Website Company called Airbnb, or "Both STR and Airbnb Experience" if you have both work experiences.



Interviews start soon. We are seeking a reliable and organized Guest Services Coordinator to assist with guest inquiries, schedule maintenance tasks, and collaborate with our housekeeping team to ensure a seamless guest experience; for our short term rental property management company that services approximately 30 listings.



We are looking for someone who can work exclusively with our team, rather than juggling multiple clients at the same time. Employees are expected to share their screen during their work shift. We realize this is out of the question for some, and that is the very reason we have that as a requirement. The persons that have a problem with this are the ones we need to screen for. If you have a problem with this, please save you and us both the time and not apply.



The ideal candidate will have excellent communication skills and a proactive approach to problem-solving. If you thrive in a demanding, fast-paced environment and are passionate about hospitality, we want to hear from you! Management potential/training/advancement and long term opportunity for the right individual. Interview schedule will be during the day during Central Time in the US, as will your work shift if you are hired. In your response confirm that you understand the time zone and your ability to work during those hours; please let us know whether or not you have experience working that shift, or that this will be the first time. The time shift is 8 AM - 4 or 5 PM Central Time, five to five and a half days per week, with the half day probably a weekend day. The most flexible candidates go to the front of the line. 



Please note for interview scheduling, Daylight Savings Time starts at midnight on Sunday, March 8, at 2:00 AM Central Time. Keep that in mind for scheduling in the coming days.



Please follow all of the instructions in above mentioned posting for your application to be considered. We will only respond to those candidates that take the time to apply because they truly see a potential fit with us.  We are expecting approximately 1000 responses to this posting, as we received that level of response the last time we hired for a position like this; this is why we have rigid rules/instructions in our screening process. In the interview process you will have the opportunity to meet and speak with the person we hired in 2025, that answered this same job posting, who has since been promoted to Management. 



Lastly, in your response,

Please rate yourself on a scale of 1-10, with 10 being the highest, on the following:

1. Basic Math skills (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, percentages, use of calculator)

2. Organization skills

3. Administration skills

4. Office/Business Communication (also tell us what you believe good office communication is)

5. Attention to detail

6. Problem solving skills

7. Technical skills (computer tools/software)

8. Adaptability (learning new tools, handling changing priorities, adjusting to different processes)

9. Online research skills

title: Social Media Specialist
description:
Job Title



Social Media Content Editor & Visual Creative



Role Summary



We are looking for a high-quality social media and video/image editor based in the Philippines who is proficient in English, highly organized, creative, and able to work with minimal supervision. This person will help turn ideas, raw footage, and brand direction into polished social media content that is engaging, on-brand, and ready to post.



This role is ideal for someone who is strong in short-form video editing, graphic design for social posts, content formatting, and visual storytelling.



Key Responsibilities



Edit short-form videos for social media platforms such as Instagram,
, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and LinkedIn



Create image-based social media posts, carousels, thumbnails, and simple branded graphics



Add captions, text overlays, transitions, music, cuts, and formatting that improve engagement



Repurpose long-form or raw content into multiple short-form assets



Follow brand guidelines, tone, and style while also bringing creative ideas to improve content performance



Organize files, drafts, and finished assets in a clean and reliable way



Deliver content on time with minimal revisions needed



Communicate clearly in English regarding deadlines, feedback, and project updates



Ideal Candidate



Based in the Philippines



Strong written and spoken English



Skilled in tools such as CapCut, Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut, Canva, Photoshop, or similar



Experienced in editing for social media performance, not just general video editing



Understands hooks, pacing, subtitles, retention editing, and platform-specific formatting



Can take a general direction and execute without needing constant oversight



Detail-oriented, responsive, and dependable



Has a strong portfolio of social media editing work



Success Metrics / KPIs



Use these to measure whether they are actually performing well:



Production Metrics



On-time delivery rate: 95%+ of assignments delivered by deadline



Revision rate: Average of 2 or fewer revision rounds per asset



Output volume: Number of completed videos/posts per week



Turnaround time: Average time from assignment to first draft



Quality Metrics



Approval rate: % of first drafts approved with minimal edits



Brand consistency: Posts match brand style, tone, and visual standards



Attention to detail: Low frequency of spelling, formatting, caption, or export errors



Performance Metrics



Engagement support: Content contributes to stronger views, watch time, shares, saves, or comments



Retention quality: Videos have strong pacing and hooks that improve watch-through rates



Repurposing efficiency: Ability to turn one source asset into multiple usable content pieces



Communication / Ownership Metrics



Responsiveness: Replies within agreed working hours



Independence: Able to complete work with minimal hand-holding



Reliability: Follows instructions, manages files properly, and communicates blockers early
